source.title: China issues rules to regulate pricing practices of internet platforms (China Daily)

extractions.claim: China's 29-article regulation banning '全网最低价' (lowest price across the internet) practices takes effect April 10, 2026
extractions.evidence: 29-article regulation jointly issued by NDRC, SAMR, and Cyberspace Administration of China. Takes effect April 10, 2026. Key prohibitions: (1) platforms cannot leverage dominant scale to impose 'lowest price' agreements on merchants, (2) cannot use traffic throttling, search ranking demotions, or algorithm penalties to pressure merchants into predatory price-cutting, (3) cannot force exclusive pricing arrangements, (4) cannot set different prices based on user data without explicit consent, (5) must clearly label advertisements in search results. Platforms must conduct self-inspections before effective date.

verification.verifier_notes: China Daily (state media), Yahoo News, SCMP all report same provisions and April 10 date. Three-agency joint regulation.

extractions.claim: SAMR finalized Antitrust Compliance Guidelines for Internet Platforms on Feb 13, 2026, identifying '全网最低价' as one of eight monopoly risk scenarios
extractions.evidence: On February 13, 2026, SAMR released finalized Antitrust Compliance Guidelines for Internet Platforms. Eight risk scenarios identified: algorithmic collusion, '二选一' (pick-one-from-two), '全网最低价' (lowest price across internet), excessive pricing, sales below cost, blocking/shielding, and AI-powered price manipulation. Guidelines demand platforms conduct 'targeted screening and dynamic monitoring of core algorithmic models, including pricing algorithms, recommendation systems, ranking and advertising placement strategies.'

verification.verifier_notes: SAMR official release per Guancha, BRICS Competition, Global Times. Draft from Nov 2025, finalized Feb 2026.

extractions.claim: The regulation directly targets PDD's core pricing mechanism: algorithmic enforcement of lowest prices and merchant price discipline
extractions.evidence: Platforms previously forced merchants to create 'different product variants with distinct model numbers across platforms' to maintain price parity, increasing operational costs significantly. The regulation bans this practice. SAMR and MoC also separately directed PDD Holdings to fix its 'refund-only' practice. China's 2026 market regulation priorities explicitly include curbing 'cutthroat competition' — a term targeting excessive price wars that erode margins and innovation. Pinduoduo's pricing algorithm that auto-matched or undercut competitor prices by fractions of a yuan is the exact behavior these rules target.

verification.verifier_notes: SCMP reported PDD directed to fix refund-only. China Daily confirmed cutthroat competition as 2026 priority. The regulation's provisions map directly to PDD's known pricing practices.
